data_in.description: Python 3.x environment with pip package manager and git access to levitsky/pyteomics repository

data_out.description: Successful import of pyteomics.mass.unimod module with no errors and instantiated Unimod accessor object ready for use

domain_knowledge: sqlalchemy is a required conditional dependency for the pyteomics.mass.unimod module that provides database abstraction and ORM functionality for accessing the Unimod proteomics database.
Python package installation via pip must complete successfully before import statements will resolve the installed sqlalchemy library.
The Unimod database accessor in pyteomics requires sqlalchemy to initialize; absence of this dependency will raise an ImportError or AttributeError at instantiation time.
Pyteomics supports recent Python 3 versions; compatibility with older Python 2.x or deprecated Python 3 releases is not guaranteed.

direct_checks: verify file exists: sqlalchemy package installable via pip in the current Python environment
script_runs: Python script that executes 'import pyteomics.mass.unimod' completes without ImportError or ModuleNotFoundError
script_runs: Python script that instantiates the Unimod database accessor (pyteomics.mass.unimod.Unimod()) completes without exception
file_exists: sqlalchemy is listed in pyteomics package dependencies or optional extras (setup.py, pyproject.toml, or requirements file)
contains_substring: pyteomics.mass.unimod module source code contains conditional import or feature-gating logic for sqlalchemy dependency
expert_review: Verify that the conditional dependency wiring correctly handles sqlalchemy availability and gracefully reports missing dependency if sqlalchemy is absent
Confirm that Unimod database accessor initialization logic is sound and does not mask upstream sqlalchemy import failures
